publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 13, 2025 at 22:01

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Susan Lominska, 2023, in memory of her aunt Katharine Ketcham (MSN, Yale class of 1939).

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Katharine Ketcham was born in 1915 in East Orange New Jersey. She graduated from Mount Holyoke College in 1936 and received her Master of Nursing in 1939 from Yale School of Nursing (YSN). She served during World War II as a Captain Army Nurse in New Zealand attached to the US 39th General Hospital, Yale Unit. After the war she returned to Connecticut and taught at YSN. She received her M.A. in Nursing Education from New York University in 1951 and continued teaching at YSN and at Hartwick College School of Nursing until she retired in 1975. Ketcham died in 1999.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Katharine Ketcham collection. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection includes materials related to Katharine Ketcham (1915-1999), Yale School of Nursing class of 1939. Items include a manuscript diary dated July 1942-September 1944 written by Ketcham about her World War II experiences: training at Camp Edwards, travelling across the United States and awaiting assignment; voyage to New Zealand and experiences while serving as an RN, captain rank, attached to the United States Army 39th General Hospital, Yale Unit. Also included is a copy of a typewritten transcript of the diary created by Ketcham's sister, Jean Ketcham, circa 1998; photographs taken by Ketcham or others in New Zealand during her service; a panoramic photograph of the Yale 39th Hospital Unit; and photographs taken by Ketcham or others at the Yale School of Nursing in 1952 while she was teaching students from the YSN class of 1954.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leaf 1-168 (336 pages), handwritten in blue ink on loose sheets of lined paper. Diary pages were housed in small vinyl binders by family members after Ketcham's death.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leaf 169-282 + 2 leaves of conversions (230 pages total) handwritten in blue ink on loose sheets of lined paper. Diary pages were housed in small vinyl binders by family members after Ketcham's death.

did.unittitle: Transcript of diary, volumes I and II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Spiral bound photocopy of diary transcript typed by Katharine Ketcham's sister Jean Ketcham Lominska circa 1998. Transcript includes message from Lominska on first page.

did.unittitle: Photographs, US Army 39th General Hospital, Yale Unit, New Zealand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 102 photographs, images include military weddings; hospital facilities, wards, and grounds; medical unit staff at work and off duty; and landscapes. Also includes one exterior photograph of Eleanor Roosevelt visiting the medical unit. Many photographs include notes and names written in pencil on the back of prints.

did.unittitle: Panoramic photograph of US Army 39th General Hospital, Yale Unit, staff

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to this item is restricted until preservation treatment is completed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 49 photographs of Yale School of Nursing staff and students participating in lessons including administering injections, applying bandages, preparing food, and preparing equipment. Many photographs include names written in pencil on the back of prints. The note on the back of one photograph reads "For Miss Ketcham from the class of 1954, Feb. 1952."
